mechanism_narrative: Rising real consumer spending (RSAFS) signals robust final demand, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month growth vs 20-day forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the decile spread between high and low RSAFS growth quintiles is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above a low baseline (0.5%) signals a shift toward monetary tightening that increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, producing negative SPX returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 0.5% after being below 0.25% for at least 3 months
regime_hint: transition from ultra-low rates to normalization
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows that 20-day SPX returns are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) in the 3 months following each FEDFUNDS > 0.5% trigger event, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and consumer spending reflects broad-based economic momentum that boosts corporate profits and lifts equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The composite signal (equal-weighted z-score of INDPRO and RSAFS 3-month growth) has a Spearman rank correlation below 0.05 with subsequent 60-day SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is elevated above 5%, further job market deterioration (rising UNRATE) signals a contracting economy and depresses equity prices; the signal is reversed in tight labor markets.
failure_condition: The conditional rank IC of UNRATE changes (1-month diff) vs 20-day forward returns when UNRATE > 5% is not negative (i.e., IC >= 0) over any 3-year rolling window, or the hit rate of negative returns exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production over the past month signals expanding factory output and economic health, driving positive short-term equity returns within the following week.
failure_condition: The weekly (5-trading-day) forward return decile spread between top and bottom INDPRO monthly growth deciles is not positive over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts below 1 million units (annualized) signals a weakening residential construction sector that drags on GDP and corporate confidence, leading to negative equity returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: In months following HOUST < 1000, the average 20-day SPX return is not negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.10), or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55% across all such events.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates (rising CPIAUCSL) while the Fed keeps rates extremely low (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%), negative real interest rates erode purchasing power and create policy uncertainty, weighing on equity returns over the next month.
regime_hint: low rate environment with rising inflation
failure_condition: The joint trigger yields an average 20-day SPX return that is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window, or the proportion of positive returns exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: In a period of stable or falling unemployment (non-recessionary labor market), further declines in UNRATE signal a tightening labor market that leads to wage pressure and Fed tightening concerns, ultimately producing negative forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
regime_hint: mature expansion with UNRATE below 6% and declining
failure_condition: The rank IC of UNRATE changes (one-month difference) vs 60-day forward SPX returns in the regime (UNRATE < 6% and 3-month declining trend) is not negative (IC >= 0) over any 3-year rolling window, or the decile spread of returns across UNRATE change buckets is not negative.
